summary: First testing iteration for Chameleon Adaptive Honeypot — both backend (FastAPI + WS + LLM) and frontend (React SOC dashboard). Ran 16 pytest cases covering all REST endpoints, WebSocket snapshot/stats stream, and all 3 real LLM endpoints (chat, lure, report) — 16/16 PASSED. Frontend loads cleanly on the preview URL, all 23 required data-testid hooks render across Command Center, Sessions, Deception, Reports, CTF. Live world map renders SVG with attack arcs & pulsing dots; event feed & MITRE heatmap stream; session replay, honeypot graph, lure modal, report controls, CTF submit+leaderboard, and AI Analyst drawer all work. LLM integrations return real Claude Sonnet 4.5 output (verified in backend tests + visible markdown response in AI drawer screenshot).

ui_bugs.issue: React console warnings: 'Encountered two children with the same key' for event uuids. Cause: WS snapshot on connect + REST /api/events hydrate both populate the same events; subsequent attack_event WS push also re-adds events that are already in state via addEvent without de-duplication. Recommend de-duping by id in addEvent (and applySnapshot/hydrate merge) to prevent duplicate keys.

React hydration warning: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>'. The Emergent dev-overlay x-* instrumentation wraps dynamic option labels with a <span style=display:contents>, which HTML forbids inside <option>. Use a plain string child (e.g. {a.label} without any surrounding expression that could be wrapped) or disable instrumentation for that expression.

action_items: (LOW) Dedupe events by id in /app/frontend/src/store/socStore.js addEvent/applySnapshot/hydrate to silence React duplicate-key warnings.
(LOW) Fix <option> label rendering in /app/frontend/src/pages/CTF.jsx so dev-overlay doesn't inject <span> children inside <option>.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 690 lines mixing simulator, models, routes, WS, LLM; acceptable at MVP but consider splitting into modules (routes/, sim/, llm/) once features grow.
predict endpoint uses Math-style randomness on every call — fine for demo, but consider caching the prediction per session so frontend panel values stop flickering if re-fetched.
No rate limiting on /api/chat, /api/lure/generate, /api/report/generate — a malicious client could burn through the LLM key. Add simple per-IP throttling.
CTF score submit accepts negative points silently; add Pydantic validator `points >= 0`.
Event/session state is in-memory only — OK for demo but not horizontally scalable; document this limitation.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all testids + all flows working; 2 React console warnings, no functional failure)

seed_data_creation: CTF leaderboard entries with TEST_ prefix created during tests (TEST_tester_<ts>, TEST_uiuser); chat_messages rows with test-sess-chat-1 session_id; one report doc in db.reports.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully green. WS endpoint at /api/ws (wss://<host>/api/ws). LLM calls use emergentintegrations with Claude Sonnet 4.5 and typically respond in 5-12s (set >=30s timeouts). Map dots in WorldMap are NOT plain <svg><circle>; they appear to be positioned divs/SVG path circles — query_selector('[data-testid=globe-container] circle') returns 0. To click an attacker on the map use the session-list approach or inspect the actual rendered element. attacker-panel testid is only present in DOM after a session is selected (or could be always present but empty). AI Analyst drawer uses an <input> placeholder 'Ask the analyst…', not a <textarea>.
rca of the issue: Duplicate React key warning RCA: socStore seeds events from both WS 'snapshot' event and REST /api/events hydrate, plus every 'attack_event' WS push calls addEvent which prepends without checking if the id already exists. When an event arrives twice (snapshot + subsequent WS broadcast or hydrate overlap), the list has two items with the same key. Mitigation: in addEvent -> if (get().events.some(x => x.id === e.id)) return; same guard in applySnapshot and hydrate merges.
